summary: An excerpt from Bill Gates' memoir 'Source Code' detailing his early childhood, his relationship with his grandmother, and how learning to play cards taught him foundational lessons about logic, probabilities, and understanding the world.

principles.rationale: No matter how complex or mysterious a system or situation seems, you can figure it out by applying your mind, observing patterns, and learning the underlying skills.
principles.source_quote: Card playing taught me that no matter how complex or even mysterious something seems, you often can figure it out. The world can be understood.

principles.statement: A life well lived requires using your brain and staying engaged.
principles.rationale: Absorbed from his grandmother's example, he believes that integrity, fairness, and continuous mental engagement are core components of a good life.
principles.source_quote: A life well lived meant living simply, giving your time and money to others, and, most of all, using your brain—staying engaged with the world.

mental_models.name: State Machine
mental_models.description: A mental algorithm that receives an input and, based on the state of a set of conditions, methodically works through probabilities, decision trees, and game theory to take the optimal action.
mental_models.example: Observing his grandmother play cards, where she would calculate odds and sometimes sacrifice a good card early to set up a win down the road.

heuristics: Think smart: use your brain and stay focused to figure out the right move.
Use physical movement, like rocking, as a metronome to focus your brain.

signature_quotes.text: Card playing taught me that no matter how complex or even mysterious something seems, you often can figure it out. The world can be understood.
signature_quotes.context: Reflecting on the lessons he learned from trying to beat his grandmother at card games.

signature_quotes.text: In computer science there’s a thing called a state machine, a part of a program that receives an input and, based on the state of a set of conditions, takes the optimal action. My grandmother had a finely tuned state machine for cards...
signature_quotes.context: Comparing his grandmother's card-playing strategy to computer science concepts.

signature_quotes.text: Rocking was like a metronome for my brain. It still is.
signature_quotes.context: Describing his childhood habit of rocking to focus his energy and thoughts.
